rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LORNA GANLEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Procedural issues with Stephen Ganley's petition: 1) No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Form DE-147S) has been filed (San Francisco Local Rule 14.18.E). 2) No summons has issued or been served. When a will is contested under Section 8004, the contestant shall file an objection to probate of the will and a summons shall be issued and served (Probate Code Section 8250(a)). 3) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ESTHER J. PAVLOFF
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/15/2026. The following issues must be cleared before 4/7/2026: (1) A copy of the lost will being offered to probate must be submitted separately. Please see Local Rule 14.20. (2) Also, the proposed order must include a copy of the lost will being offered to be admitted. Prob. Code sec. 8223. (3) Petition #8 states that all trust beneficiaries are listed; however, it is not clear that everyone whose name is listed is a trust beneficiary: Doris, Douglas, William, Carol, David, Ronald, Paula, Daniel, and Christina. Or only a few of them are the trust beneficiaries. Need clarification. (4) The petition states Ronald's whereabouts are unknown, but notice was sent to him at a certain address. If the petitioners found Ronald, the petition needs to be updated. A verified supplement should be filed. (5) If David Demers's issues cannot be identified and/or located, the Court will likely need a declaration of due diligence search stating what was done to locate them. (6) Paula Kovacs is listed as a niece by marriage, but the petition does not clearly explain how she is related, either. (7) Each petitioner submitted a declaration regarding the possibly lost will, but their statements appear to support the presumption of Prob. Code sec. 6124 rather than overcoming it.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JANICE WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/28/2026. The following issues need to be cleared before 4/17/2026: (1) The original will must be submitted. If this nondomiciliary decedent's will was admitted in another state (Texas), an authenticated copy of the will and an authenticated copy of the order admitting the will to probate in that court must be conventionally (not e-filing) submitted. (2) In Petition #8, clearly state Wanda's and Wilma's relationship to the decedent. If they qualify for Notice, provide them with the remote instruction as required by the Local Rule. (3) File the Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number [Form DE-147S] separately. Refer to Local Rule SF 14.18.E and 14.59.C. (4) The proof of publication must state that the petition requests admission of the decedent's will. If it does not, republication is required. (5) The petition is missing information regarding the following points: (6) #5.a. (3)-(4) regarding surviving registered domestic partner. (7) #2.d regarding bond. A minimum bond of $20,000 is required for a non-California resident personal representative under Local Rule 14.16.E. File a verified supplement requesting the minimum bond. (8) In Petition #3.d, specify whether the $600,000 refers to income from real property or to an ownership interest in real property. (9) In Petition 3.f.(2), indicate whether the decedent's will is self-proving. If it is not, file a Proof of Subscribing Witness.
